title: aira pulchella subsp. intermedia
intro: Aira pulchella subsp. intermedia, also known as the lesser meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain. Aira pulchella subsp. intermedia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
